LM_Notes: Post-2006 coup surge. 4800+ webpages blocked. iLaw/TLHR data.
Court_Notes: PM Samak banned (cooking show). Coalition dissolved Dec 2008. 109 executives banned. Led to Abhisit PM.

LM_Notes: Continued high enforcement under Abhisit government. Multiple cases tied to Red Shirt movement.
Court_Notes: No major anti-government rulings. Abhisit (Democrat) in power with establishment support.

LM_Notes: Wikipedia/iLaw: 478 cases in 2010. Peak year. Red Shirt crackdown. 60,000 websites banned.
Court_Notes: No major rulings. Red Shirt crackdown handled by military, not courts.

LM_Notes: Remained high early year. Declined after Yingluck took office Aug 2011.
Court_Notes: Yingluck won landslide July. No immediate court action.

LM_Notes: Declined significantly under Yingluck. She said she would not seek to reform the law but enforcement dropped.
Continued low under Yingluck. Conviction rate ~76% (UN OHCHR).
Court_Notes: Court ruled against constitutional amendment attempt by Yingluck government.

LM_Notes: May 2014 coup. NCPO granted military tribunal authority. 127 arrested May 2014-Jul 2017. Spike post-coup.
Court_Notes: PM Yingluck removed May 2014 (national security chief transfer). Coup followed.

LM_Notes: Junta enforcement. 60-year sentence for Facebook posts (Pongsak). Conviction rate jumped to ~96%.
Court_Notes: Junta in power. No need for court to act against government.

LM_Notes: King Bhumibol died Oct 2016. Spike in prosecutions late year. Jatupat arrested for sharing BBC article.
Court_Notes: Junta in power. Court approved new constitution.

LM_Notes: Multiple major cases (Pai Dao Din, Somsak Facebook share cases). TLHR documented several.
Court_Notes: Junta in power. New constitution took effect.

LM_Notes: TLHR: No new Section 112 cases prosecuted in 2018. Courts dismissed several existing cases. Dramatic drop.
Court_Notes: Junta in power. No oppositional rulings needed.

LM_Notes: Enforcement remained low. Post-election period. Prayuth continued as PM via Senate.
Court_Notes: Thai Raksa Chart dissolved (Feb). Prayuth ruled eligible to continue as PM (Sep).

LM_Notes: Low until Nov 2020. Prayuth pledged to enforce 'all laws' against pro-democracy protesters in late Nov.
Court_Notes: Future Forward Party dissolved (Feb). Thanathorn banned 10 years.

LM_Notes: Massive surge. 259 individuals charged Nov 2020-Oct 2023 per FIDH. 43-year sentence handed down.
Court_Notes: No major dissolutions. Protest prosecutions via criminal courts, not Constitutional Court.

LM_Notes: Continued high enforcement against pro-democracy movement. FIDH/iLaw tracking.
Court_Notes: Prayuth suspended Aug (term limit case). Returned Sep after favorable ruling.

LM_Notes: 100 verdicts reached by Oct 2023 (FIDH). 79% conviction rate. Arnon Nampa convicted Sep 2023.
Court_Notes: MFP blocked from forming government. Pita suspended for media shares case.

LM_Notes: Move Forward dissolved Aug 2024 for advocating Section 112 reform. Srettha removed Aug 2024.
Court_Notes: Move Forward dissolved Aug 7. PM Srettha removed Aug 14. Two major rulings in one week.

LM_Notes: Paetongtarn removed Aug 2025. Focus shifted to border conflict. Enforcement continued but attention diverted.
Court_Notes: PM Paetongtarn removed Aug 29 after Hun Sen phone call crisis.
